title: ranunculus retroflexus
intro: Ranunculus retroflexus, also known as poison ivy, is a perennial plant native to North America, characterized by its shiny leaves and greenish-yellow flowers. The plant contains urushiol, an oily sap that can cause skin irritation and allergic reactions in some individuals. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, and its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antipyretic properties. Herbal preparations of Ranunculus retroflexus may include teas, infusions, and ointments, but due to its toxic properties, it is essential to use caution and consult with a healthcare professional before using this plant for medicinal purposes.
